leadText: Piloswine (Japanese: イノムー Inomoo) is a dual-type Ice/Ground Pokémon introduced in Generation II. It evolves from Swinub starting at level 33 and evolves into Mamoswine when leveled up while knowing Ancient Power.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Piloswine's eyes and feet Piloswine is a big, brown, furry porcine Pokémon with an impressive hump on its back. It also has two tusks made of ice, which seem small but can be lethal. These tusks thicken when it snows, and they are smaller on a female Piloswine than on a male. It has floppy ears that are sensitive to sound. Piloswine has two beady eyes in its head; however, these are usually covered up by tufts of fur. As it is usually unable to see, it checks surroundings with its sensitive pig-like nose instead. As a result, Piloswine is constantly charging at foes when it moves. The hairs on its back stands up straight as a warning sign for this. Piloswine's thick fur allows it to survive in harshly cold environments that it inhabits, such as icy caves , mountainous regions and frozen tundra. Its fur is so long that it usually covers its small, hooved legs. Despite the small size of its legs, its hooves ensure even footing on icy ground. Piloswine often digs for food buried beneath snow and ice using its tusks. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Piloswine evolves from Swinub and evolves into Mamoswine . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Swinub Ice Ground → First Evolution Piloswine Ice Ground → Second Evolution Mamoswine Ice Ground

(Pokémon Blue, Gold, or Yellow inserted) Generation III Hoenn #— Kanto #— Ruby Piloswine is covered by a thick coat of long hair that enables it to endure the freezing cold. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry DP104 Piloswine Dawn's Pokédex Piloswine, the Swine Pokémon and the evolved form of Swinub . Piloswine's entire body is covered with fur, which makes it hard to see, so it uses its nose to sense its environment instead.
